name: Big O Complexity Growth Comparison
description:
This visualization illustrates the rate of growth for common Big O time complexities used in algorithm analysis. As the input size n increases, the number of operations required by different algorithms diverges significantly.

  • O(1) & O(log n): Excellent efficiency, remaining nearly flat even as data grows.
  • O(n): Linear growth, typical of a single pass through a collection.
  • O(n log n): Efficient sorting algorithms like Merge Sort or Quick Sort.
  • O(n²): Quadratic growth, common in nested loops (e.g., Bubble Sort).
  • O(2ⁿ): Exponential growth, where adding one element doubles the work, quickly becoming computationally infeasible.
The chart is capped at a Y-value of 400 to show the intersection points and the 'explosion' of exponential and quadratic curves relative to efficient ones.
